core_misconception.description: Player sorts the cards by visual position, by where a card happens to start, or by card color or size, without actually computing the addition expression on each card and comparing the resulting values.

forbidden_scope: exactly three cards per round in this first real run
each card shows a simple addition expression with single-digit values summing to within 10
ordering rule must be stated in plain text before the round starts
ordering rule must remain visible for the whole round
no timer pressure in this first real run
no scoring layer in this first real run
an incorrect commit must not penalize the player; it must only jiggle-and-reset for another attempt
notes: Backfilled by scripts/backfill_project_config.py from legacy artifacts for slug snack-line-shuffle-mini. Source summary: concept_brief.real.json + concept_packet.draft.json + concept_packet.manifest.json. CLI overrides: none. This file was derived from existing artifacts that pre-date Package 10; it must be reviewed before being treated as authoritative operational state.
subject: math
success_metric: The player places all three cards in the correct smallest-to-largest order on first commit, demonstrating that they computed each expression value and compared them rather than guessing by position or appearance.

target_skill.description: Compute small single-digit addition expressions mentally, compare the resulting values, and rank-order three items from smallest to largest without guessing from visual position or card appearance.
